title: Selector
description: Addresses a span within a source's recorded representation — how observation.created, observation.declined's candidate_span_selector, and evidence.* relate a claim back to exact bytes (SPEC.md invariant 9: every claim resolves to a source URI and selector). Modeled on the W3C Web Annotation TextQuoteSelector/TextPositionSelector pattern: a quote is robust to re-fetch drift, an offset is exact against one fixed representation — carrying both means a stale offset is still recoverable.

representation_id.description: The exact fetched/computed representation these coordinates are relative to (observation.representation.computed) — never the live URI, which can change underfoot.

text_position.description: Exact character offsets into representation_id's plain-text form. Present when kind is text_position, and recommended alongside text_quote even when kind is text_quote, as the fast path when the representation hasn't drifted.

text_quote.description: Exact text plus surrounding context, re-locatable even if offsets have drifted (e.g. the source was re-fetched and normalized differently).

media_region.description: Non-text span — a time range, a spatial region, or both, for audio/video/image sources.
